meta.title: California Anniversary Road Trip
meta.subtitle: From the Golden Gate to the Las Vegas lights
meta.route: Chicago → San Francisco → Yosemite → Monterey → Big Sur → Santa Barbara → Los Angeles → San Diego → Joshua Tree → Death Valley → Las Vegas → Chicago
meta.dataNote: Prices and availability are planning snapshots checked Jul 11, 2026, not reservations.

days.theme: First taste of San Francisco — Bay, bridge and cable car ✈️🌉

sections.heading: Morning
items: Schedule-fit flight: United ORD 6:00 AM → SFO 8:44 AM, nonstop, $237 total for 2 before bags on Booking.com
Easier MDW alternative: Southwest 10:00 AM → SFO 12:50 PM, nonstop, $236.80–$237 total for 2; this makes the sightseeing window tighter

sections.heading: Midday
items: Take BART into the city and leave luggage at the Marina/Fisherman's Wharf hotel
Lunch near the waterfront; see Flight Options for all live fare choices and baggage rules

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: 2:30 PM — Pier 39 and the sea lions only if the flight is on time
4:00–5:05 PM — Golden Gate Bridge; Dec 10 sunset is about 4:50 PM, not 6:00 PM
If arrival is delayed, protect the bridge sunset and move Pier 39 to the evening

sections.heading: Evening
items: 6:00 PM — Dinner in North Beach or Chinatown
7:30 PM — Historic Powell–Hyde cable car from Powell & Market toward Hyde & Beach; prepay with MuniMobile or Clipper

days.stayNote:
Francisco Bay Inn — Booking.com
8.0★ · $178.70 total ($89.35/night)
Book →
days.ticketNotes:
• Powell–Hyde cable car (SFMTA)

sections.heading: Before the day
items: Prebook a guided Muir Woods/Sausalito round trip, a reserved seasonal shuttle, or a taxi with a guaranteed return
Do not rely on summoning an on-demand rideshare from Muir Woods: dependable cell service is not available and the shuttle is seasonal

sections.heading: Morning
items: 8:30 AM — Breakfast at the hotel or a local café
9:30–11:30 AM — Lands End coastal trail (~3 mi, ocean cliffs and old ruins)

sections.heading: Midday
items: Around noon — Depart using the confirmed Muir Woods transportation plan
Cathedral Grove / main redwood loop at a quiet pace

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: Continue to Sausalito if included in the reserved transport plan
Return to San Francisco using the guaranteed ride or a confirmed ferry connection

sections.heading: Evening
items: 6:30 PM — Dinner and Mission murals or North Beach jazz
Early night before the SFO rental pickup and Yosemite drive

days.stayNote:
— Same hotel as previous night —
(San Francisco, see Day 1)
days.ticketNotes:
• Muir Woods transport guidance (NPS)
• Muir Woods parking / shuttle reservations

days.drive: SFO Rental Car Center → Yosemite View Lodge (El Portal) via Merced · 200 mi · 3 hr 33 min baseline; allow 4.5–5 hrs after pickup and in winter
days.theme: Airport pickup, then city to snowy Sierra 🚗🏔️

sections.heading: Morning
items: 6:15 AM — Check out with luggage and travel back to SFO by BART or a prebooked ride
Use the 24-hour AirTrain Blue Line from the terminals/BART station to the SFO Rental Car Center
8:00 AM — Pick up the SUV at the airport counter, matching the rental shortlist and quote times
Confirm the contract says AWD, inspect tires and obtain written traction-device guidance before leaving
8:30–9:00 AM — Depart via Merced, CA-99 and Hwy 140

sections.heading: Winter gate
items: Yosemite requires chains/cables to be carried when controls are active, including in AWD rentals
If controls require equipment the rental agreement or assigned vehicle does not permit, do not install unauthorized devices: use YARTS from the El Portal/Yosemite View Lodge corridor for the park day

sections.heading: Midday
items: Lunch and fuel in Merced; recheck Yosemite road and chain controls

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: Aim to reach El Portal around 2:00–3:00 PM, depending on pickup, traffic and weather
Check in and keep Tunnel View optional unless daylight and roads are comfortable

sections.heading: Evening
items: Dinner in El Portal; save the YARTS timetable and prepare layers and a day pack

sections.heading: Morning decision
items: 7:30 AM — Check road and chain controls before moving the SUV
Drive into Yosemite Valley only when the assigned vehicle and rental agreement legally satisfy the current control level
If not, board YARTS from the El Portal/Yosemite View Lodge corridor and leave the rental parked

sections.heading: Morning
items: 8:00 AM — El Portal → Yosemite Valley, ~15 mi / ~30–35 min each way in normal conditions
9:00–11:30 AM — Valley Loop highlights: El Capitan Meadow, Bridalveil Fall and Cook's Meadow

sections.heading: Midday
items: Picnic or Yosemite Village lunch; carry food, water and warm layers

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: 1:30–3:15 PM — Mirror Lake easy trail or a slower Valley-floor walk
3:45 PM — Tunnel View for golden-hour light only if transport, road and parking conditions are comfortable

sections.heading: Evening
items: Return to El Portal before full darkness if weather is deteriorating
Dinner and an early night before the Monterey transfer

days.drive: Yosemite View Lodge (El Portal) → Monterey via Gilroy · 190 mi · 3 hr 42 min baseline; allow 4–4.5 hrs

sections.heading: Morning
items: 8:00 AM — Breakfast and road-condition check
8:30 AM — Depart El Portal for Monterey via Hwy 140 and Gilroy

sections.heading: Midday
items: Lunch and fuel in Gilroy; keep the stop short enough to arrive before dark

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: Around 2:00–3:00 PM — Arrive Monterey and check in
Cannery Row or the waterfront at an easy pace
Optional 17-Mile Drive only if you arrive early with good daylight; otherwise keep it on the anniversary day

sections.heading: Evening
items: Seafood dinner near Fisherman's Wharf
Early night before the anniversary activities

days.theme: Otters, whales, and the coast that made you fall for California 🦦🥂

sections.heading: Morning
items: 8:00 AM — Breakfast
9:00–11:00 AM — Gray-whale watching boat trip (book morning slot, calmer seas) — ~$50–75pp, see ticket links →

sections.heading: Midday ($60–90pp)
items: 11:30 AM — Sea-otter kayaking tour in Monterey Bay, Cannery Row — calm water, otters and sea lions up close, see ticket links →
1:30 PM — Lunch on Cannery Row

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: 3:00 PM — Point Lobos Reserve (Monterey → Point Lobos, ~4 mi, ~10 min) — short cliff trails, dramatic coastline
4:30 PM — Drive the 17-Mile Drive scenic loop toward Carmel (~17 mi loop, ~45 min with stops)

sections.heading: Evening — ★ Anniversary
items: 5:30 PM — Sunset at a Carmel viewpoint or Point Lobos overlook
6:00 PM — Walk Carmel-by-the-Sea's storybook village, browse galleries
7:30 PM — Anniversary dinner — book ahead, worth the splurge tonight

days.stayNote:
— Same hotel as previous night —
(Monterey — Munras Ave, see Day 5)

days.drive: Monterey → Bixby Creek Bridge → Elephant Seal Vista Point → Cambria · 102 mi · 2 hr 55 min driving only

sections.heading: Morning
items: 8:00 AM — Fuel up, recheck Caltrans QuickMap and depart Monterey
Use US-101 as the fallback if Highway 1 closes; recheck 24 hours before and again that morning
Bixby Creek Bridge photo stop

sections.heading: Midday
items: Cap the day at three meaningful stops, with slow scenic pullouts and a picnic lunch
McWay Falls Overlook Trail remains closed into 2026: no trail or beach access
Only take a legal roadside glimpse if parking and traffic are clearly safe; skip it if full

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: Continue to Elephant Seal Vista Point near San Simeon
Aim to reach Cambria around 4:00 PM; Dec 16 sunset is about 4:53 PM

sections.heading: Evening
items: Moonstone Beach boardwalk or Cambria Christmas Market
Dinner and early night for the first Hearst tour tomorrow

sections.heading: Morning
items: 7:30 AM — Breakfast and checkout
8:10 AM — Drive Cambria → Hearst Castle Visitor Center
8:35 AM — Arrive at least 25 minutes before a first tour around 9:00 AM
Reserve the tour in advance; use the official link in this row

sections.heading: Midday
items: Around 10:30–11:00 AM — Depart San Simeon for Santa Barbara
Lunch in San Luis Obispo or Santa Maria

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: Around 2:30–3:30 PM — Arrive Santa Barbara and check in
State Street or Mission Santa Barbara at a relaxed pace

sections.heading: Evening
items: Stearns Wharf sunset and dinner nearby

sections.heading: Morning
items: 9:00 AM — Slow breakfast, no alarm
10:00 AM — Beach time — East Beach or Butterfly Beach

sections.heading: Midday (free)
items: 12:30 PM — Casual lunch, no plans
1:30 PM — Optional easy walk: Douglas Family Preserve bluffs (ocean views, flat trail)

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: 3:00 PM — Wander the Funk Zone (wineries, breweries, art) or just relax at the hotel

sections.heading: Evening
items: 6:00 PM — Early, easy dinner
Pack for tomorrow's move to LA

days.drive: Santa Barbara → Los Angeles (Culver City) · ~95 mi · ~2 hrs
days.theme: Into Los Angeles — Route 66 meets the Pacific 🚗🌴

sections.heading: Morning
items: 9:00 AM — Breakfast and depart Santa Barbara for Culver City (~95 mi / ~2 hrs)

sections.heading: Midday
items: 11:30 AM — Check in or leave luggage at the Culver City hotel
12:30 PM — Lunch nearby

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: 2:00 PM — Santa Monica Pier: find the Route 66 “End of the Trail” sign and take the centennial photo
2026 is Route 66's 100th anniversary; the Chicago-to-Santa Monica connection makes this a meaningful American road-trip moment
Ferris wheel / boardwalk, then a 4:00 PM Venice Beach and canals walk

sections.heading: Evening
items: 6:30 PM — Dinner on the west side
Keep the evening easy before the Hollywood studio day

days.theme: Step inside Hollywood, then see it from above 🎬✨

sections.heading: Morning
items: 7:15 AM — Breakfast and depart Culver City with traffic buffer
8:00 AM — Arrive at Warner Bros. Studio Tour Hollywood; bring government-issued photo ID
8:30–11:30 AM — Working-studio tour and self-guided exhibits (~3 hrs; current adult price $79)

sections.heading: Midday
items: 11:45 AM — Lunch in Burbank, Los Feliz or Hollywood
1:15–2:15 PM — TCL Chinese Theatre forecourt / short tour and a focused Walk of Fame visit
Do not turn Hollywood Boulevard into a long checklist

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: 2:30–3:15 PM — Travel to Griffith Observatory and park or use the shuttle
3:30 PM onward — Observatory, Hollywood Sign and city views through the roughly 4:45 PM sunset

sections.heading: Evening
items: Stay for the first city lights
6:30–7:30 PM — Dinner in Los Feliz or Hollywood
Getty Center is intentionally removed; this day now reflects the traveler's stronger interest in Hollywood

days.drive: Culver City → Cabrillo National Monument → Sunset Cliffs → San Diego · 146 mi · 3 hr 16 min baseline; allow 4+ hrs with traffic
days.theme: Arrive with the tide, not against it 🌊🚗

sections.heading: Morning
items: 7:30–8:00 AM — Depart Culver City; avoid a leisurely LA morning today
Drive directly toward Point Loma with one short rest stop

sections.heading: Midday
items: Target Cabrillo arrival around 12:15–12:30 PM
12:30–3:00 PM — Tidepool window around the predicted 1:33 PM low tide (−0.94 ft)
Recheck NOAA and NPS access 24–48 hours beforehand; swell, weather or staffing can still close the area

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: Around 4:00 PM — Sunset Cliffs Natural Park
Check into the San Diego hotel after sunset

sections.heading: Evening
items: Dinner in Point Loma, Old Town or Little Italy; keep the evening light

days.theme: Sea caves and an aircraft carrier ⛵✈️

sections.heading: Morning
items: 8:30–10:30/11:00 AM — La Jolla sea-cave kayaking; use the calmer morning slot

sections.heading: Midday
items: 11:30 AM — Leave La Jolla for the Embarcadero

sections.heading: Afternoon

sections.heading: Evening

items: Buy/print the park pass and download the official NPS offline map
Fill the tank and pack breakfast, lunch, water and warm layers; services are limited

sections.heading: Morning
items: 6:30 AM — Depart San Diego and drive directly toward Cholla Cactus Garden

sections.heading: Midday
items: Around 10:45–11:30 AM — Cholla Cactus Garden short loop
Picnic lunch, then continue west through Joshua Tree

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: Choose one main western stop: Hidden Valley OR Keys View if running behind
Aim for Keys View by roughly 4:10 PM only if roads, daylight and energy allow

sections.heading: Evening
items: Check into Yucca Valley for one night
Early dinner and pack for a 7:30 AM departure

days.drive: Yucca Valley → Furnace Creek · 256 mi · 4 hr 03 min baseline; allow 4.5–5 hrs
days.theme: One direct transfer, then sunset inside Death Valley 🎄🏜️

sections.heading: Morning — Christmas Eve
items: 7:00 AM — Breakfast, full tank, extra water, food and downloaded offline maps
7:30 AM — Depart directly for Furnace Creek via CA-247 and CA-127
Do not detour back to Cholla or Palm Springs; that would add about 74 miles and 1 hr 50 min

sections.heading: Midday
items: Carry lunch and extra water; holiday services may close early
Keep the rental on paved roads only; skip dirt spurs such as Natural Bridge and Devil's Golf Course
Aim to reach Furnace Creek around noon to 1:00 PM

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: Visitor Center, check-in and a short nearby viewpoint
Zabriskie Point near sunset if energy and road conditions are good

sections.heading: Evening
items: Dinner at the resort area; reserve ahead for Christmas Eve
Sleep inside Death Valley, eliminating the late drive to Pahrump

days.drive: Furnace Creek → Badwater Basin → Artist's Palette → Zabriskie Point → Las Vegas Strip · 156 mi · 3 hr 05 min driving only
days.theme: Death Valley highlights, then Christmas lights in Las Vegas 🏜️✨

sections.heading: Morning — Christmas Day
items: Carry breakfast/lunch, a backup meal and extra water; do not rely on every park service being open
8:00 AM — Badwater Basin
9:45 AM — Artist's Drive and Artist's Palette

sections.heading: Midday
items: Return toward Furnace Creek; use carried supplies or a confirmed-open resort outlet
Call the Oasis about one week beforehand to confirm Christmas dining hours
Zabriskie Point if you did not visit at sunset yesterday

sections.heading: Afternoon
items: Around 1:30–2:00 PM — Depart for Las Vegas
The mapped route is 156 mi / about 3 hr 05 min before sightseeing time

sections.heading: Evening
items: Check into the Strip hotel and keep Christmas dinner reserved
8:15 or 8:30 PM — Bellagio fountains after dinner; allow 20–30 minutes and check for weather or schedule changes
Everything after the fountains remains optional

days.drive: Las Vegas Strip → Harry Reid International Airport (LAS) · 2.6 mi · ~10 min driving; allow 30–45 min to rental return

sections.heading: Recommended budget path
items: United LAS 7:00 AM → ORD 12:46 PM, nonstop: $281 total for 2 on Booking.com or $285.57 on Expedia in Basic Economy
For a standard carry-on, seat choice and changes, Expedia showed regular United Economy at $387.27 total before checked bags
Southwest LAS 8:10 AM → MDW 1:35 PM is the simpler carry-on/MDW alternative at about $377–$381 total for 2

sections.heading: Airport sequence if booking the 7:00 AM United flight
items: About 3:45 AM — Leave the Strip hotel after checking out the night before
Fuel and return the SUV; allow 30–45 minutes for the rental return and shuttle
Reach the terminal with the airline's recommended domestic check-in buffer

sections.heading: Midday
items: Arrive Chicago around 12:46 PM local time

sections.heading: Important
items: The cheapest return fares remove the previous “sleep-in” morning; choose a later, higher fare if a relaxed final morning matters more than savings
Reconfirm price, baggage, airport and schedule immediately before booking

budget.notes: Live base-fare floor is about $518–$522 for 2 across both one-way legs. Allowance includes realistic baggage/carry-on/seat tradeoffs; see Flight Options. Prices checked Jul 11, 2026.

Airport-only SFO→LAS SUV allowance. Avis exact-date live totals are $2,768.94–$2,934.22; the higher ceiling allows for a written AWD class. Excludes optional protection, fuel and tolls. See Rental Options.
Fuel up before Big Sur, Joshua Tree and Death Valley — plan ~1,650–1,800 mi including local driving
Low = practical budget picks, including the Ranch inside Death Valley; dated quotes checked Jul 11, 2026. Parking remains TBD.

budget.category: Activities (kayak, whales, Hearst, USS Midway, Warner Bros.)
budget.notes: Adds two Warner Bros. Studio Tour tickets at the current $79 adult price and the cable-car ride; Route 66 sign, Bellagio fountains and tidepools are free.

rental.bestFit: Explicitly labeled gasoline Midsize SUV AWD — Nissan Rogue AWD or similar
rental.guidance: Start with Enterprise, National or Alamo. Hertz is the strongest 24-hour backup; Avis provides the exact-date price benchmark.

vendors.bestUse: Fast pickup via Emerald Aisle eligibility
Best late-arrival backup with AWD classes

checklist: Reservation must say AWD/4WD in the class name or class code; a generic SUV reservation is not enough.
At SFO, inspect the assigned vehicle for AWD markings and M+S-rated tires on all four wheels before leaving.
Get written confirmation about traction-device permission and vehicle clearance. Yosemite may require carrying chains even in AWD.
Confirm unlimited mileage, one-way fee, taxes/airport fees, cancellation terms, and the final LAS return address.
Photograph every panel, wheel, windshield, fuel level, and odometer at pickup and again at return.
If chain controls are active and the rental agreement does not permit devices, park before the control point and use YARTS.

flights.pick: United ORD 6:00 AM → SFO 8:44 AM, then United LAS 7:00 AM → ORD 12:46 PM
flights.realisticTotal: $710–$950 for two after baggage, seats and fare flexibility

planningGates.suggestion: Treat the coast as the day, not a transfer packed with stops.
planningGates.guardrail: Depart about 8 AM, cap at three meaningful stops, reach Cambria before sunset.

planningGates.suggestion: Closures can turn a famous stop into wasted time and unsafe roadside behavior.
planningGates.guardrail: Trail/beach removed. Legal roadside glimpse only if safe; otherwise skip.

planningGates.suggestion: Use a lower-elevation base/approach and make chain control a hard gate.
planningGates.guardrail: Hwy 140 approach; confirm AWD, tire size and written chain/cable compatibility at pickup. If the rental cannot legally meet controls, park in El Portal and use YARTS.
planningGates.timing: 48h before + SFO pickup + morning-of; save YARTS schedule

planningGates.suggestion: Schedule the coast around the low tide, not the other way around.
planningGates.guardrail: 12:15–12:30 arrival remains well aligned to the 1:33 PM low tide.

planningGates.suggestion: Traverse once; carry essentials because services and connectivity are limited.
planningGates.guardrail: Pass bought/printed, offline map, full tank, food and water added to Day 14.

planningGates.suggestion: An inside-park night improves sunrise/sunset access and removes a late desert drive.
